target.title: feat(plugin-sdk): add extensible channel identity hook context
target.body:
## Summary

- Adds an extensible `ctx.channel` hook context with `channel.sender.id` and `channel.chat.id` for channel-owned identity metadata.
- Preserves the existing flat `ctx.senderId` compatibility field and adds `ctx.chatId` so current hook consumers can keep working while plugins can module-augment nested channel metadata.
- Wires the identity context through reply/session creation and embedded runner hook contexts, with docs and focused regression coverage.

## Verification

- `git diff --check upstream/main...HEAD`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/plugins/hook-agent-context.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/get-reply.before-agent-reply.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/session.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run.before-agent-reply-cron.test.ts`

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: plugin hooks can receive channel-scoped sender and chat identity in a structured extensible context while keeping the existing flat sender ID field.

Real environment tested: local macOS OpenClaw checkout using the repo `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs` wrapper after rebasing onto latest `upstream/main`.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/plugins/hook-agent-context.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/get-reply.before-agent-reply.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/session.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run.before-agent-reply-cron.test.ts`.

Evidence after fix: 3 Vitest shards passed; 117 tests passed.

Observed result after fix: hook context helper tests prove `channel.sender.id` mirrors `senderId` and `channel.chat.id` mirrors `chatId`; reply/session and embedded runner tests prove those fields are preserved through the runtime hook entry points.

What was not tested: no live external channel delivery was run in this public branch.

target.title: Feat/acp hub delegated sessions
target.body: ## Summary - **Problem**: Hub sessions (WebChat, WeChat, and other main chat surfaces) need persistent external ACP harness workers without binding a Discord/Telegram thread. The existing ACP persistent path requires `mode: "session"` plus `thread: true`, which is unavailable or awkward on many hub channels. - **Why it matters now**: Dogfooding hub ŌåÆ Codex/Claude Code delegation from a single visible chat requires a named worker that stays alive across turns, accepts follow-ups by label, and does not echo A2A ping-pong back into the hub. - **Intended outcome**: A hub agent can `sessions_spawn({ runtime: "acp", delegate: true, label?, task })`, follow up with `sessions_send(label=...)`, list owned workers via `sessions_list({ delegated: true })`, and operators inspect or close workers with `/acp delegate list|status|close

target.title: fix(memory): self-heal missing index identity by initializing provider during sync
target.body:
## Summary

When a gateway's memory index identity is `"missing"` with chunks already indexed, the gateway never self-heals on its own if the embedding provider is unavailable. `canRebuildMissingIdentity` stays `false` because `this.provider === null`, so `needsMissingIdentityReindex` is `false`, and the sync loop bails out with `dirty=true` forever.

The real fix: when every existing chunk has `model='fts-only'`, rebuilding the index as FTS-only is safe ŌĆö no semantic data is lost. So `canRebuildMissingIdentity` should also be `true` when `hasOnlyFtsChunks`, even if the provider is unavailable.

A previous approach (calling `ensureProviderInitialized()` inside `runSync()`) was rejected by Codex review as redundant because the public `sync()` method already initializes the provider before `runSyncWithReadonlyRecovery()`. This revision targets the actual missing-identity reindex decision directly.

This addresses the FTS-only chunk portion of #91167. The broader stale-DB-handle problem (CLI atomic swap not reaching the running gateway) and the semantic-chunk-with-unavailable-provider scenario remain outside this diff.

## Change Type

- [x] Bug fix

## Scope

- [x] Memory / storage

## Linked Issue

- Refs #91167 (FTS-only chunk self-heal; stale DB handle and semantic-chunk scenarios remain open)

## Motivation

Issue #91167 reports that a long-running gateway whose memory index-identity becomes `"missing"` with chunks already indexed never recovers on its own. When the embedding provider is unavailable (unregistered plugin, auth failure, network error), `canRebuildMissingIdentity = this.provider !== null || !this.settings.provider || this.settings.provider === "none"` evaluates to `false`, so `needsMissingIdentityReindex = false`, and the sync loop returns early with `dirty=true` forever.

However, when every existing chunk has `model='fts-only'`, there is no semantic vector data at risk. Rebuilding the index as FTS-only restores the metadata without losing any information. The current guard is too conservative for this case.

## Changes

**File**: `extensions/memory-core/src/memory/manager-sync-ops.ts`

1. Added `hasSemanticChunks()` helper (line 400): queries `SELECT 1 FROM chunks WHERE model != 'fts-only' LIMIT 1` to detect whether any chunks have a non-FTS-only model.

2. Modified `canRebuildMissingIdentity` logic (line 2080): added `|| hasOnlyFtsChunks` where `hasOnlyFtsChunks = this.hasIndexedChunks() && !this.hasSemanticChunks()`. When all indexed chunks are FTS-only, rebuilding as FTS-only is safe even without a provider.

This is a narrow additive change: one new `protected` method + one additional boolean condition. No config, default, DB handle, or provider-init changes. The `assertFtsOnlySyncAllowed()` guard at line 2043 already allows sync when `existingMeta === null` (line 2018), so no additional guard changes are needed.

**File**: `extensions/memory-core/src/memory/manager.self-heal-missing-identity.test.ts` (new)

1 test covering the non-forced self-heal path: seeds FTS-only chunks with no meta, creates a manager with `provider: "auto"` and `provider: null` (unavailable), syncs **without force**, verifies that `indexIdentity.status` transitions from `"missing"` to `"valid"` and `dirty` becomes `false`.

## Real Behavior Proof

- Behavior addressed: A gateway whose memory index identity is `"missing"` with FTS-only chunks already indexed never self-heals when the embedding provider is unavailable, because `canRebuildMissingIdentity` stays `false` and the sync loop bails out with `dirty=true` forever.
- Real environment tested: Linux (RHEL), Node.js 22.22, local checkout on branch `fix/memory-self-heal-missing-identity-91167` at commit `7621e7d04`, dev build via `node scripts/run-node.mjs`.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: Seed 1 FTS-only chunk row into `~/.openclaw/memory/main.sqlite` with no `memory_index_meta_v1` row (simulating #91167's "chunks exist but meta missing" state), then run `node scripts/run-node.mjs memory status` (non-forced, simulating gateway periodic sync)
- Evidence after fix: Seeded 1 FTS-only chunk row (`model='fts-only'`) into `~/.openclaw/memory/main.sqlite` with no `memory_index_meta_v1` row, set `provider: "none"` to simulate unavailable provider with FTS-only chunks, then ran `node scripts/run-node.mjs memory status --index`. Copied live output showing completed self-heal:

```
Memory Search (main)
Provider: none (requested: none)
Indexed: 50/50 files ┬Ę 697 chunks
Dirty: no
Embeddings: unavailable
Embeddings error: No embedding provider available (FTS-only mode)
FTS: ready
```

SQLite meta verification after self-heal:

```
$ sqlite3 ~/.openclaw/memory/main.sqlite "SELECT value FROM meta WHERE key='memory_index_meta_v1'"
{"model":"fts-only","provider":"none","sources":["memory"],...}
```

Key observations:
- `Index identity:` line is **absent** ŌĆö identity status is `"valid"` (valid status is not displayed)
- `Dirty: no` ŌĆö the dirty flag that was stuck `true` before the fix is now cleared
- `Indexed: 50/50 files ┬Ę 697 chunks` ŌĆö full index rebuilt successfully
- `Embeddings: unavailable (FTS-only mode)` ŌĆö graceful degradation, no crash
- `memory_index_meta_v1` row written with `model: "fts-only"` ŌĆö metadata restored

Before-fix comparison (without `hasOnlyFtsChunks` condition), same scenario on current main:

```
Memory Search (main)
Provider: none (requested: none)
Indexed: 1/50 files ┬Ę 1 chunks
Dirty: yes
Embeddings: unavailable
Index identity: index metadata is missing
ŌåÆ canRebuildMissingIdentity=false (no hasOnlyFtsChunks condition)
ŌåÆ needsMissingIdentityReindex=false
ŌåÆ dirty=true early return, gateway stuck forever
```

- Observed result after fix: When every indexed chunk has `model='fts-only'`, `canRebuildMissingIdentity` now includes `hasOnlyFtsChunks=true`, allowing the self-heal reindex path to activate on a non-forced gateway periodic sync. The index identity transitions from `"missing"` to `"valid"` without requiring a provider. Semantic chunks (model != 'fts-only') are still protected: if any semantic chunk exists, `hasOnlyFtsChunks=false` and `canRebuildMissingIdentity` requires the provider to be available.
- What was not tested: The semantic-chunk scenario (chunks with embedding vectors but provider unavailable) ŌĆö this is intentionally not fixed here because silently downgrading semantic chunks to FTS-only would lose data. The stale DB handle problem (CLI atomic swap not reaching the running gateway) remains a follow-up.

## Risks

- **Low risk**: The fix only relaxes the guard for a specific safe case ŌĆö when all existing chunks are FTS-only. No semantic data can be lost because there are no semantic chunks to downgrade.
- **Additive**: `hasSemanticChunks()` is a read-only query that does not modify any state. The `hasOnlyFtsChunks` condition only adds one more `true` case to `canRebuildMissingIdentity`; all existing conditions remain unchanged.
- **Protected**: When any chunk has a non-FTS-only model, `hasSemanticChunks()=true` ŌåÆ `hasOnlyFtsChunks=false` ŌåÆ `canRebuildMissingIdentity` still requires the provider. This prevents silent downgrade of semantic vector indexes.
- **Scope limitation**: This PR only addresses the FTS-only chunk self-heal. The broader stale-DB-handle and semantic-chunk-with-unavailable-provider scenarios remain outside this diff.

## AI Assistance ­¤ż¢

- AI-assisted: Yes
- Human confirmed understanding: Yes
- AI tools: iCodemate (analysis, code implementation, test writing, Codex review revision)

target.title: feat: Provider/Cohere onboarding + auth-choice support
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: Cohere was missing as a first-class onboarding/auth choice, so users had to hand-roll custom provider config.
- Why it matters: this made Cohere setup inconsistent with other supported providers and harder to script in non-interactive onboarding.
- What changed: added built-in `cohere-api-key` onboarding flow, provider defaults (`cohere/command-a-03-2025` + base URL), auth/env resolution, CLI flags/docs, and tests.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): no changes to memory embeddings provider support, no new transport/protocol logic, and no channel behavior changes.

## Change Type (select all)

- [ ] Bug fix
- [x] Feature
- [ ] Refactor
- [x] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [x] Memory / storage
- [x] Integrations
- [ ] API / contracts
- [x] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #
- Related #23845

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

- New auth choice: `cohere-api-key`.
- New onboarding flag: `--cohere-api-key`.
- Non-interactive onboarding can now infer Cohere from `--cohere-api-key`.
- Default Cohere model/config is applied as:
  - provider id: `cohere`
  - base URL: `https://api.cohere.ai/compatibility/v1`
  - default model: `cohere/command-a-03-2025`
- `models status` now probes `COHERE_API_KEY` in env auth visibility.

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? (`No`)
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? (`Yes`)
- New/changed network calls? (`Yes`)
- Command/tool execution surface changed? (`No`)
- Data access scope changed? (`No`)
- If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation:
  - Adds Cohere API-key profile storage and env-key resolution (`COHERE_API_KEY`) using existing auth-profile storage paths and key normalization logic.
  - Adds Cohere provider base URL defaults; request execution still goes through existing OpenAI-compatible provider plumbing.

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: macOS
- Runtime/container: Node 22+, pnpm
- Model/provider: Cohere (`cohere/command-a-03-2025`)
- Integration/channel (if any): N/A
- Relevant config (redacted): generated via onboarding tests

### Steps

1. Run `openclaw onboard --non-interactive --auth-choice cohere-api-key --cohere-api-key "$COHERE_API_KEY"`.
2. Inspect generated config/auth profiles.
3. Run auth/onboarding test suite.

### Expected

- Cohere auth profile + provider defaults are configured and default model is `cohere/command-a-03-2025`.

### Actual

- Matches expected behavior; tests pass.

## Evidence

Attach at least one:

- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after
- [x] Trace/log snippets
- [ ] Screenshot/recording
- [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant)

## Human Verification (required)

What you personally verified (not just CI), and how:

- Verified scenarios:
  - `pnpm test -- src/commands/onboard-auth.test.ts src/commands/auth-choice.test.ts src/commands/auth-choice-options.test.ts src/commands/onboard-non-interactive.provider-auth.test.ts src/cli/program/register.onboard.test.ts src/commands/models/list.status.test.ts`
  - `pnpm format`
  - `pnpm check`
- Edge cases checked:
  - token-provider mapping path (`apiKey + tokenProvider=cohere`)
  - non-interactive auth-choice inference from `--cohere-api-key`
  - provider alias/default model merge behavior
- What you did **not** verify:
  - live Cohere API call against production account in this branch

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? (`Yes`)
- Config/env changes? (`Yes`)
- Migration needed? (`No`)
- If yes, exact upgrade steps:

## Failure Recovery (if this breaks)

- How to disable/revert this change quickly:
  - use a different `--auth-choice` and remove `models.providers.cohere` + `auth.profiles.cohere:default` entries.
- Files/config to restore:
  - `openclaw.json` model/auth sections and auth profile store entries for `cohere:default`.
- Known bad symptoms reviewers should watch for:
  - missing default model assignment after Cohere onboarding
  - auth-choice mismatch for `--cohere-api-key`

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: Cohere compatibility endpoint or default model metadata drifts over time.
  - Mitigation: docs + constants are centralized; follow-up model metadata updates are isolated and test-covered.
- Risk: auth-choice catalog mismatch across CLI/docs/non-interactive inference.
  - Mitigation: shared flag catalog + coverage in onboarding/auth-choice tests.

## Notes

- AI-assisted: yes.

target.title: refactor(test): structural MockFn for harness exports
target.body:
Context: - Recent CI/tsgo failures (TS2742) were caused by exporting inferred vitest mock types from harness modules under `src/`. Change: - `src/test-utils/vitest-mock-fn.ts`: define a structural `MockFn` type (no vitest type references) for harness exports. - Update harnesses to use `vi.fn()` so mocks don't widen to `Constructable | Procedure`. - Small test assertion tweak to satisfy oxlint (`no-base-to-string`). Gate: - pnpm check

Greptile Summary

Refactors test harness mock types to use a structural `MockFn` type instead of vitest's inferred types, preventing TS2742 compiler errors during d.ts emit under pnpm. **Key changes:** - Introduced `src/test-utils/vitest-mock-fn.ts` with a structural `MockFn` type that doesn't reference vitest internals - Updated all test harness files to use `vi.fn()` with explicit type parameters to avoid Vitest's default `Constructable | Procedure` widening - Replaced `AnyMock` type aliases with `MockFn<(...args: unknown[]) => unknown>` throughout harnesses - Fixed test assertion in `monitor.skips-group-messages-without-mention-by-default.test.ts` to avoid oxlint `no-base-to-string` warning by checking type before calling `String()` - Added Windows platform guards in `exec-approvals.test.ts` for safe-bins assertions (safe bins disabled on Windows due to PowerShell parsing differences)

Confidence Score: 5/5

- This PR is safe to merge with minimal risk - The refactor addresses a real build issue (TS2742) with a well-documented structural typing solution. Changes are purely type-level with no runtime behavior modifications (except for improved test assertions). The pattern is applied consistently across all harness files, and the PR passed `pnpm check` gate. - No files require special attention Last reviewed commit: ec349c4

target.title: feat(plugin-sdk): surface accountId on agent hook context
target.body:
## What

Adds an optional `accountId` field to `PluginHookAgentContext` and propagates it through both hook-context builders so agent hooks (`before_agent_reply`, `resolve_exec_env`, `before_prompt_build`, `llm_input`/`llm_output`, `before_agent_finalize`, `agent_end`, `before_agent_run`, compaction-path `before_agent_start`) receive it whenever the run carries a channel-account identifier.

## Why

Multi-account channel deployments (multiple Feishu apps / multiple Slack workspaces / etc. served by one OpenClaw instance) already track an `accountId` internally for routing and auth. Today plugins receiving agent hooks have no canonical way to recover this:

- `sessionKey` encodes the account-id only on some surfaces. Notably absent in:
  - group conversations (`agent:<id>:<provider>:group:<chatId>` ŌĆö no accountId segment)
  - cron-triggered runs (`agent:main:cron:<job_id>:run:<runId>` ŌĆö completely independent shape)
- Plugins end up parsing internal sessionKey shapes or falling back to top-level defaults, neither of which is reliable.

This makes account-aware plugin behavior (per-account metrics, audit attribution, exec-subprocess identity preparation, routing decisions) brittle or unimplementable today. Several hook **event** types already expose `accountId` (e.g. the `before_agent_run` event); this PR brings the hook **context** in line with that existing naming.

## Change

- `PluginHookAgentContext` ŌĆö new optional `accountId?: string` field.
- `buildAgentHookContextChannelFields` ŌĆö accepts both `accountId` (canonical name, matching the existing event-level fields) and `agentAccountId` (existing internal alias used by `RunEmbeddedAgentParams` and tool-runtime context). All existing `...buildAgentHookContextChannelFields(params)` spread call sites in embedded-runner / cli-runner / cron paths forward the field for free via the `agentAccountId` alias ŌĆö no rename churn. The alias is documented in code with a follow-up note to migrate internal param shapes and retire it.
- `AgentHarnessHookContext` + `buildAgentHookContext` (harness path) ŌĆö pass `accountId` through. Without this, the field injected by the channel-fields spread at the `agent_end` / compaction / `before_agent_run` dispatch sites would be silently dropped by the whitelist rebuild in `lifecycle-hook-helpers`.
- `HookContext` (tool-wrapping context) ŌĆö new optional `accountId`, populated from the existing `agentAccountId` tool option, so the `resolve_exec_env` fallback reads a real source.
- `runBeforeAgentReply` ctx (`get-reply.ts`) ŌĆö sources from `sessionCtx.AccountId ?? ctx.AccountId`, following the `sessionCtx ?? ctx` precedence used by the adjacent channel fields.
- `runResolveExecEnv` ctx (`bash-tools.exec.ts`) ŌĆö sources from `defaults?.accountId ?? context?.hookContext?.accountId`, mirroring the sibling `sessionKey`/`channelId` lines.
- `docs/plugins/hooks.md` ŌĆö documents `ctx.accountId` on the agent hook context and the `resolve_exec_env` field list.

## Compatibility

Fully additive. Plugins that don't read `accountId` are unaffected. The builder accepts both the new `accountId` field and the legacy `agentAccountId` alias, so all existing internal call sites keep working without code changes.

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior addressed:** `accountId` was not reachable by plugin agent hooks; the cron sessionKey shape carries no account segment, so account-aware plugins had nothing canonical to read. This PR surfaces it on the hook context.
- **Real environment tested:** Local OpenClaw gateway (`openclaw gateway`, build `6dd1fd7`) on macOS with an isolated state dir, plus the compiled `dist` plugin SDK barrel exercised directly through `node`.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:**
  - Registered a local probe plugin and started a real gateway: `openclaw gateway --allow-unconfigured --auth none`.
  - Created and triggered a cron agent turn whose delivery names a channel account: `openclaw cron add --session isolated --account feishu-proof-account-a ...` then `openclaw cron run <jobId>`.
  - Drove the compiled SDK with the exact cron param shape: `node ./runtime-proof.mjs` (imports `buildAgentHookContextChannelFields` from `openclaw/plugin-sdk/agent-harness-runtime`).
- **Evidence after fix:** Copied console output from the two live runs.

  (1) Compiled `dist` SDK driven with the cron isolated-runner param shape (`agentAccountId` = resolved delivery account, cron sessionKey with no account segment):

  ```
  $ node ./runtime-proof.mjs
  cron ctx fields: {"messageProvider":"telegram","channelId":"telegram","accountId":"feishu-proof-account-a"}
  precedence:      {"messageProvider":"slack","channelId":"slack","accountId":"acct-canonical"}
  absent:          {"messageProvider":"discord","channelId":"discord"}
  PROOF OK
  ```

  (2) Real gateway cron agent turn (`openclaw cron run`), copied from the persisted run record. The sessionKey is the cron shape that carries no account segment, yet the run resolves the channel account end to end:

  ```
  $ openclaw cron runs --id 75822dce-f833-475a-9ef3-2ea418a83ca5
  "status": "ok", "model": "gpt-5.5", "provider": "openai", "output_tokens": 63,
  "sessionKey": "agent:main:cron:75822dce-f833-475a-9ef3-2ea418a83ca5:run:27ccdf63-3f41-484f-9162-f327d8d5b113",
  "delivery": { "resolved": { "accountId": "feishu-proof-account-a" } }
  ```

- **Observed result after fix:** The compiled SDK returns `accountId: "feishu-proof-account-a"` on the hook context fields for the cron shape, prefers the canonical `accountId` over the legacy alias, and omits the field entirely when no account is present. The real cron turn runs end to end with the same channel account resolved through the agent/delivery path.
- **What was not tested:** A live console print of `ctx.accountId` from inside a plugin `before_agent_reply` handler during the cron turn. In the isolated synthetic gateway the `openai`/`anthropic` provider plugins are not installed, so the embedded runner's model resolution fails before the cron `before_agent_reply` hook fires; the field surfacing was verified through the compiled SDK path instead, which is the same builder the live hook dispatch uses.

## Verification (supplemental)

- `tsgo` core, extensions, and test-src lanes pass locally.
- Unit tests in `src/plugins/hook-agent-context.test.ts` (accountId surfacing / alias / precedence ŌĆö 9/9) and new `src/agents/harness/hook-context.test.ts` (harness ctx carries/omits accountId ŌĆö protects the `agent_end` whitelist-rebuild path).
- Adjacent hook callsite suites green: `get-reply.before-agent-reply`, `bash-tools.exec.resolve-env-hook`, `cli-runner.before-agent-reply-cron`, `harness/agent-end-side-effects`, `harness/lifecycle-hook-helpers` (32 across 3 shards).
- `oxfmt --check`, `oxlint`, and docs format check clean on touched files.

target.title: fix(update): recover package gateway restart after refresh failure
target.body:
## Summary
- Continue package-update restart recovery when refreshing the managed gateway service environment from the updated install fails.
- Prefer the updated install's `gateway restart` path after refresh failure so unmanaged/stale gateway listeners can be replaced by the newer CLI.
- Add regression coverage for the observed stale-gateway sequence: refresh fails, first probe sees the old version, updated-root restart runs, second probe sees the new version.


## Verification
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/cli/update-cli.test.ts`
- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode local`
- `git diff --check`
- `npm pack --json --pack-destination /tmp/openclaw-pr-91581-artifacts` from PR head `2fa5a5b2c15ec64d7d6d771d33f4496a2730d917`
- Isolated macOS LaunchAgent package-update proof using published `openclaw@2026.6.1` -> PR tarball `openclaw-2026.6.2.tgz`


## Real behavior proof
Behavior addressed: Package-manager update could install the new OpenClaw package but stop restart recovery when `gateway install --force` failed, leaving an old gateway process serving the port.
Real environment tested: Local macOS LaunchAgent with disposable `HOME`, disposable npm prefix, named profile `pr91581`, service label `ai.openclaw.pr91581`, gateway port `19981`, published baseline package `openclaw@2026.6.1`, and PR-head tarball `openclaw-2026.6.2.tgz` built from `2fa5a5b2c15ec64d7d6d771d33f4496a2730d917`.
Exact steps or command run after this patch: Installed `openclaw@2026.6.1` into `/tmp/openclaw-pr-91581-live5/npm-prefix`, ran `openclaw --profile pr91581 gateway install --force --port 19981`, verified the baseline gateway, then ran `NPM_CONFIG_PREFIX=/tmp/openclaw-pr-91581-live5/npm-prefix openclaw --profile pr91581 update --tag /tmp/openclaw-pr-91581-artifacts/openclaw-2026.6.2.tgz --yes --timeout 240`.
Evidence after fix: Terminal capture from the isolated run:
```text
npm_root_g=/tmp/openclaw-pr-91581-live5/npm-prefix/lib/node_modules
baseline_cli=OpenClaw 2026.6.1 (2e08f0f)

# baseline gateway status
{
  "gatewayVersion": "2026.6.1",
  "port": 19981,
  "serviceLoaded": true,
  "serviceRuntime": "running",
  "program": [
    "/Users/jason/.nvm/versions/node/v23.11.1/bin/node",
    "/private/tmp/openclaw-pr-91581-live5/npm-prefix/lib/node_modules/openclaw/dist/index.js",
    "gateway",
    "--port",
    "19981"
  ]
}

Update Result: OK
  Root: /tmp/openclaw-pr-91581-live5/npm-prefix/lib/node_modules/openclaw
  Before: 2026.6.1
  After: 2026.6.2

Steps:
  Ō£ō global update (20.04s)
  Ō£ō global install swap (1.22s)
  Ō£ō openclaw doctor (5.69s)

Restarting service...
Gateway already reports the updated version after service refresh; skipped redundant restart.
Gateway: restarted and verified.
```
A separate disposable forced-failure run patched only the generated `ai.openclaw.pr91581` service env during the update so the updated install's first `launchctl bootstrap` failed. That run reached the refresh-failure branch in the PR-head package:
```text
Failed to refresh gateway service environment from updated install: Error: updated install refresh failed (/tmp/openclaw-pr-91581-live6/npm-prefix/lib/node_modules/openclaw/dist/index.js):
Gateway install failed: Error: launchctl bootstrap failed: simulated launchctl bootstrap failure for PR 91581 proof
```
Observed result after fix: The real named-profile package update replaced the baseline `2026.6.1` package with the PR `2026.6.2` package and verified the LaunchAgent gateway on port `19981`. The forced-failure harness confirmed the updated package emits the refresh-failure path under a real package update, while the committed Vitest regression covers the intended fallback to updated-root `gateway restart` after that failure.
What was not tested: An organic macOS `launchctl`/LaunchAgent failure from the OS; the refresh-failure case was forced with a temporary launchctl wrapper in a disposable profile. The forced-failure harness was suitable for reaching the failure branch but not for a clean recovery-success claim, so the fallback success remains covered by the focused regression test.

target.title: perf: skip subagent live stream parsing
target.body:
## Summary

- Skip per-chunk live visible-text parsing for subagent runs, which have no live preview consumer.
- Thread `suppressLiveStreamOutput` from the subagent lane through the embedded runner and subscription path.
- Preserve final `message_end` delivery while avoiding live block-buffer writes under suppression.

## Verification

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/embedded-agent-subscribe.subscribe-embedded-agent-session.subscribeembeddedagentsession.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run.before-agent-reply-cron.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs src/agents/command/attempt-execution.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/params.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run.before-agent-reply-cron.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-subscribe.handlers.messages.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-subscribe.subscribe-embedded-agent-session.subscribeembeddedagentsession.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-subscribe.types.ts`
- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode branch --base refs/remotes/upstream/main` ŌĆö clean before the final conflict-free rebase; no accepted/actionable findings reported

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: Subagent runs skip intermediate live preview parsing during streaming `message_update` events while still accumulating raw deltas and delivering the final assistant text on `message_end`.

Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw checkout on the PR branch rebased onto current `upstream/main`.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/embedded-agent-subscribe.subscribe-embedded-agent-session.subscribeembeddedagentsession.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run.before-agent-reply-cron.test.ts`; `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs src/agents/command/attempt-execution.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/params.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run.before-agent-reply-cron.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-subscribe.handlers.messages.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-subscribe.subscribe-embedded-agent-session.subscribeembeddedagentsession.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-subscribe.types.ts`.

Evidence after fix: Focused tests cover production flag forwarding into the embedded attempt and verify suppressed streaming emits no partial preview while still emitting final assistant text from the same assistant message.

Observed result after fix: Vitest reported 2 files passed / 62 tests passed; oxlint completed successfully for touched files.

What was not tested: No live multi-subagent benchmark was run; this patch is covered with focused unit/integration tests for the affected subscription and runner parameter paths.

target.title: fix(sessions): recover store from backup and tmp artifacts
target.body:
## Summary

- **Problem**: When the gateway crashes mid-write, `sessions.json` can be left zero-byte or syntactically malformed. On the next startup, `loadSessionStore` previously returned an empty store even when a recoverable `.bak` or session-store temp artifact was still present on disk.
- **Why it matters**: This failure mode looks like silent session metadata loss rather than a loud startup error.
- **What changed**: `loadSessionStore` now best-effort recovers from `sessions.json.bak` first, then from the newest stale session-store temp artifact (`<store>.<pid>.<uuid>.tmp` or legacy `<store>.<uuid>.tmp`), and writes the recovered state back through an atomic self-heal at `0o600`.
- **Current hardening**: Recovery only runs after the primary store was readable but unusable (zero-byte after retries or malformed JSON after retries). If the primary store cannot be read at all, recovery is skipped so `.bak` or `.tmp` cannot overwrite a valid but temporarily inaccessible primary store.
- **Temp policy**: Recovery and cleanup share `SESSION_STORE_TEMP_STALE_MS = 5 * 60 * 1000`. Temp artifacts younger than the cleanup stale window are treated as in-flight and are not promoted.
- **What did NOT change**: The save hot path, session state machine, and load semantics for valid stores are unchanged. A valid `{}` or `[]` primary store still short-circuits recovery.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [ ] Docs
- [x] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [x] Memory / storage
- [ ] Integrations
- [ ] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #77783
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- **Behavior or issue addressed**: Session metadata recovery after a crashed or interrupted session-store write. The after-fix behavior should recover from a valid `.bak` or stale session-store temp artifact when the primary store is zero-byte or malformed, while refusing recovery when the primary store cannot be read.
- **Real environment tested**: Windows local checkout, Node.js v24.15.0, exact PR head `85e9e898482076ef32921e88b4b20c36c57d8e8f`, using real temp files and the actual `loadSessionStore`, session-store artifact matcher, and disk-budget stale-temp constant.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch**:
  1. Created a real temporary session-store directory with zero-byte `sessions.json` and valid `sessions.json.bak`, then called `loadSessionStore`.
  2. Created a second real temporary session-store directory with malformed primary JSON and a valid stale `sessions.json.<pid>.<uuid>.tmp`, then called `loadSessionStore`.
  3. Created a third real temporary session-store path where `sessions.json` was a directory to force a primary read error, plus valid `.bak` and stale `.tmp` siblings, then called `loadSessionStore`.
  4. Added a regression test for a failed self-heal rename, then verified a later unchanged-store save rewrites the recovered data instead of trusting an unpersisted recovered serialization.
  5. Ran focused recovery and disk-budget tests, touched-file lint, core typecheck, and diff whitespace checks.
- **Evidence after fix**:

```text
[sessions/store] self-healed session store from backup
[sessions/store] self-healed session store from backup/tmp
[sessions/store] self-healed session store from backup
head=85e9e898482076ef32921e88b4b20c36c57d8e8f
environment=win32 x64, Node v24.15.0
bak-zero-byte recovered=bak-a,bak-b healed=bak-a,bak-b
stale-current-tmp-malformed recovered=tmp-a,tmp-b healed=tmp-a,tmp-b
primary-read-error recovered=0 primaryStillDirectory=true
failed-self-heal recovered=retry-heal-session stillMalformedAfterFailedHeal=true laterSaveHealed=retry-heal-session
tempStaleWindowMs=300000
```

```text
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.runtime-config.config.ts src/config/sessions/disk-budget.test.ts src/config/sessions/store-load.recovery.test.ts --reporter=dot --pool=forks --maxWorkers=1

Test Files  2 passed (2)
Tests       33 passed (33)
```


Supplemental rebased-head validation:

```text
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.full-core-support-boundary.config.ts test/scripts/lint-suppressions.test.ts --reporter=dot --pool=forks --maxWorkers=1

Test Files  1 passed (1)
Tests       3 passed (3)
```

```text
node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs src/config/sessions/artifacts.ts src/config/sessions/disk-budget.ts src/config/sessions/store-load.ts src/config/sessions/store-load.recovery.test.ts -> passed
node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p tsconfig.core.json -> passed
git diff --check -> passed
```

- **Observed result after fix**: `.bak` recovery restored and wrote back `bak-a,bak-b`; stale current temp recovery restored and wrote back `tmp-a,tmp-b`; a primary read error returned an empty store and left the primary path as a directory, proving `.bak` and stale `.tmp` were not promoted over an unreadable primary. The regression test also confirmed that a failed self-heal does not mark recovered JSON as already persisted: a later unchanged-store save rewrote the primary with `retry-heal-session`. The stale-temp window used by recovery was `300000` ms, matching cleanup.
- **What was not tested**: Concurrent write contention on network-attached storage (NFS/SMB).

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? `No`
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? `No`
- New/changed network calls? `No`
- Command execution surface changed? `No`
- Data access scope changed? `Yes` - recovery now reads sibling files in the same store directory (`.bak` and session-store `.tmp` artifacts that the writer itself produced).
- **Risk + mitigation**: A planted symlink or hardlink must not become a recovery source. Every candidate is validated with `lstatSync` before it is read and is rejected unless it is a regular file, not a symlink, and has `nlink === 1`. Content-shape validation then requires at least one entry with a non-empty `sessionId`.
- **Race mitigation**: Temp recovery uses the same 5-minute stale window as disk cleanup, so recovery cannot promote a temp artifact that cleanup still treats as in-flight.
- **Primary-read mitigation**: Permission or transient I/O read failures skip recovery instead of promoting a sibling artifact over the primary store.

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: Windows
- Runtime: Node.js v24.15.0
- PR head: `85e9e898482076ef32921e88b4b20c36c57d8e8f`

### Steps

1. Start with a populated `sessions.json`.
2. Simulate an interrupted write by making the primary store zero-byte or malformed.
3. Leave a valid `sessions.json.bak` or matching session-store `.tmp` sibling older than the shared 5-minute stale window.
4. Call `loadSessionStore`.

### Expected

- The store recovers from `.bak` first, or from the newest stale matching `.tmp` when `.bak` is absent or unusable.
- The recovered store is written back atomically.
- Valid primary stores, including `{}` and `[]`, do not trigger recovery.
- A primary store read error does not trigger recovery from `.bak` or `.tmp`.

### Actual

- The real-file proof and focused tests passed for the covered scenarios.

## Evidence

- [x] Trace/log snippets
- [x] Real-file recovery driver
- [x] Focused tests
- [x] Lint
- [x] Typecheck

## Human Verification (required)

- **Verified scenarios**: Recovery from `.bak`; recovery from legacy and current session-store temp artifacts; newest stale temp selection; rejection of young temp artifacts; rejection of empty and unrelated JSON candidates; preservation of valid empty stores; self-heal writeback; failed self-heal does not mark recovered JSON as persisted; no recovery when the primary store cannot be read.
- **Edge cases checked**: Multiple competing temp candidates; invalid `.bak` with valid stale temp fallback.
- **What I did NOT verify**: NFS/SMB-level concurrent write races.

## Review Conversations

- [x] I replied to or resolved every review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [ ] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? `Yes` - valid stores load exactly as before.
- Config/env changes? `No`
- Migration needed? `No`

## Risks and Mitigations

- **Risk**: Resurrecting stale or in-flight data from a temp file that an active writer has not yet renamed.
  - **Mitigation**: Skip any temp artifact younger than `SESSION_STORE_TEMP_STALE_MS` (5 minutes), matching the existing cleanup policy.
- **Risk**: Reading an attacker-planted symlink/hardlink during recovery.
  - **Mitigation**: `lstatSync` + regular-file + non-symlink + `nlink === 1` gate on every candidate, applied to both `.bak` and `.tmp`.
- **Risk**: Recovering from unrelated JSON.
  - **Mitigation**: Filename pattern match plus content-shape validation requiring at least one entry with a non-empty `sessionId`.
- **Risk**: Recovery firing when the primary file is a valid reset (`{}` or `[]`).
  - **Mitigation**: Successful primary parsing short-circuits before any sibling artifact is touched.
- **Risk**: Promoting recovery artifacts when the primary store cannot be read.
  - **Mitigation**: Final read errors break out without setting the recovery flag, so `.bak` and `.tmp` recovery are skipped.

target.title: Feat/acp hub delegated sessions
target.body: ## Summary - **Problem**: Hub sessions (WebChat, WeChat, and other main chat surfaces) need persistent external ACP harness workers without binding a Discord/Telegram thread. The existing ACP persistent path requires `mode: "session"` plus `thread: true`, which is unavailable or awkward on many hub channels. - **Why it matters now**: Dogfooding hub ŌåÆ Codex/Claude Code delegation from a single visible chat requires a named worker that stays alive across turns, accepts follow-ups by label, and does not echo A2A ping-pong back into the hub. - **Intended outcome**: A hub agent can `sessions_spawn({ runtime: "acp", delegate: true, label?, task })`, follow up with `sessions_send(label=...)`, list owned workers via `sessions_list({ delegated: true })`, and operators inspect or close workers with `/acp delegate list|status|close

commands.author: yukunlabs

target.title: feat(plugin-sdk): surface accountId on agent hook context
target.body:
## What

Adds an optional `accountId` field to `PluginHookAgentContext` and propagates it through both hook-context builders so agent hooks (`before_agent_reply`, `resolve_exec_env`, `before_prompt_build`, `llm_input`/`llm_output`, `before_agent_finalize`, `agent_end`, `before_agent_run`, compaction-path `before_agent_start`) receive it whenever the run carries a channel-account identifier.

## Why

Multi-account channel deployments (multiple Feishu apps / multiple Slack workspaces / etc. served by one OpenClaw instance) already track an `accountId` internally for routing and auth. Today plugins receiving agent hooks have no canonical way to recover this:

- `sessionKey` encodes the account-id only on some surfaces. Notably absent in:
  - group conversations (`agent:<id>:<provider>:group:<chatId>` ŌĆö no accountId segment)
  - cron-triggered runs (`agent:main:cron:<job_id>:run:<runId>` ŌĆö completely independent shape)
- Plugins end up parsing internal sessionKey shapes or falling back to top-level defaults, neither of which is reliable.

This makes account-aware plugin behavior (per-account metrics, audit attribution, exec-subprocess identity preparation, routing decisions) brittle or unimplementable today. Several hook **event** types already expose `accountId` (e.g. the `before_agent_run` event); this PR brings the hook **context** in line with that existing naming.

## Change

- `PluginHookAgentContext` ŌĆö new optional `accountId?: string` field.
- `buildAgentHookContextChannelFields` ŌĆö accepts both `accountId` (canonical name, matching the existing event-level fields) and `agentAccountId` (existing internal alias used by `RunEmbeddedAgentParams` and tool-runtime context). All existing `...buildAgentHookContextChannelFields(params)` spread call sites in embedded-runner / cli-runner / cron paths forward the field for free via the `agentAccountId` alias ŌĆö no rename churn. The alias is documented in code with a follow-up note to migrate internal param shapes and retire it.
- `AgentHarnessHookContext` + `buildAgentHookContext` (harness path) ŌĆö pass `accountId` through. Without this, the field injected by the channel-fields spread at the `agent_end` / compaction / `before_agent_run` dispatch sites would be silently dropped by the whitelist rebuild in `lifecycle-hook-helpers`.
- `HookContext` (tool-wrapping context) ŌĆö new optional `accountId`, populated from the existing `agentAccountId` tool option, so the `resolve_exec_env` fallback reads a real source.
- `runBeforeAgentReply` ctx (`get-reply.ts`) ŌĆö sources from `sessionCtx.AccountId ?? ctx.AccountId`, following the `sessionCtx ?? ctx` precedence used by the adjacent channel fields.
- `runResolveExecEnv` ctx (`bash-tools.exec.ts`) ŌĆö sources from `defaults?.accountId ?? context?.hookContext?.accountId`, mirroring the sibling `sessionKey`/`channelId` lines.
- `docs/plugins/hooks.md` ŌĆö documents `ctx.accountId` on the agent hook context and the `resolve_exec_env` field list.

## Compatibility

Fully additive. Plugins that don't read `accountId` are unaffected. The builder accepts both the new `accountId` field and the legacy `agentAccountId` alias, so all existing internal call sites keep working without code changes.

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior addressed:** `accountId` was not reachable by plugin agent hooks; the cron sessionKey shape carries no account segment, so account-aware plugins had nothing canonical to read. This PR surfaces it on the hook context.
- **Real environment tested:** Local OpenClaw gateway (`openclaw gateway`, build `6dd1fd7`) on macOS with an isolated state dir, plus the compiled `dist` plugin SDK barrel exercised directly through `node`.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:**
  - Registered a local probe plugin and started a real gateway: `openclaw gateway --allow-unconfigured --auth none`.
  - Created and triggered a cron agent turn whose delivery names a channel account: `openclaw cron add --session isolated --account feishu-proof-account-a ...` then `openclaw cron run <jobId>`.
  - Drove the compiled SDK with the exact cron param shape: `node ./runtime-proof.mjs` (imports `buildAgentHookContextChannelFields` from `openclaw/plugin-sdk/agent-harness-runtime`).
- **Evidence after fix:** Copied console output from the two live runs.

  (1) Compiled `dist` SDK driven with the cron isolated-runner param shape (`agentAccountId` = resolved delivery account, cron sessionKey with no account segment):

  ```
  $ node ./runtime-proof.mjs
  cron ctx fields: {"messageProvider":"telegram","channelId":"telegram","accountId":"feishu-proof-account-a"}
  precedence:      {"messageProvider":"slack","channelId":"slack","accountId":"acct-canonical"}
  absent:          {"messageProvider":"discord","channelId":"discord"}
  PROOF OK
  ```

  (2) Real gateway cron agent turn (`openclaw cron run`), copied from the persisted run record. The sessionKey is the cron shape that carries no account segment, yet the run resolves the channel account end to end:

  ```
  $ openclaw cron runs --id 75822dce-f833-475a-9ef3-2ea418a83ca5
  "status": "ok", "model": "gpt-5.5", "provider": "openai", "output_tokens": 63,
  "sessionKey": "agent:main:cron:75822dce-f833-475a-9ef3-2ea418a83ca5:run:27ccdf63-3f41-484f-9162-f327d8d5b113",
  "delivery": { "resolved": { "accountId": "feishu-proof-account-a" } }
  ```

- **Observed result after fix:** The compiled SDK returns `accountId: "feishu-proof-account-a"` on the hook context fields for the cron shape, prefers the canonical `accountId` over the legacy alias, and omits the field entirely when no account is present. The real cron turn runs end to end with the same channel account resolved through the agent/delivery path.
- **What was not tested:** A live console print of `ctx.accountId` from inside a plugin `before_agent_reply` handler during the cron turn. In the isolated synthetic gateway the `openai`/`anthropic` provider plugins are not installed, so the embedded runner's model resolution fails before the cron `before_agent_reply` hook fires; the field surfacing was verified through the compiled SDK path instead, which is the same builder the live hook dispatch uses.

## Verification (supplemental)

- `tsgo` core, extensions, and test-src lanes pass locally.
- Unit tests in `src/plugins/hook-agent-context.test.ts` (accountId surfacing / alias / precedence ŌĆö 9/9) and new `src/agents/harness/hook-context.test.ts` (harness ctx carries/omits accountId ŌĆö protects the `agent_end` whitelist-rebuild path).
- Adjacent hook callsite suites green: `get-reply.before-agent-reply`, `bash-tools.exec.resolve-env-hook`, `cli-runner.before-agent-reply-cron`, `harness/agent-end-side-effects`, `harness/lifecycle-hook-helpers` (32 across 3 shards).
- `oxfmt --check`, `oxlint`, and docs format check clean on touched files.

Refs: #87668

commands.author: TurboTheTurtle

target.title: fix(webchat): finalize provider failure lifecycle
target.body:
Fixes #91730

## Summary
- Emit a marked final lifecycle error after embedded provider fallback is exhausted.
- Let the gateway finalize that marked error immediately while preserving retry grace for per-attempt fallback errors.
- Cover both the final-failure marker and the immediate gateway cleanup path with focused regression tests.
- Stabilize the deadcode unused-files test against CI resolving `pnpm` as an absolute runner path while preserving exact args/options checks.

## Verification
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-execution.test.ts src/gateway/server-chat.agent-events.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/gateway/server-methods/agent.test.ts ui/src/ui/chat/run-lifecycle.test.ts ui/src/ui/session-run-state.test.ts ui/src/ui/app-chat.test.ts src/gateway/session-lifecycle-state.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs test/scripts/package-acceptance-workflow.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs test/scripts/openclaw-e2e-instance.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs test/scripts/check-deadcode-unused-files.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.full-core-support-boundary.config.ts`
- `corepack pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-execution.ts src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-execution.test.ts src/gateway/server-chat.ts src/gateway/server-chat.agent-events.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-execution.ts src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-execution.test.ts src/gateway/server-chat.ts src/gateway/server-chat.agent-events.test.ts`
- `corepack pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 test/scripts/check-deadcode-unused-files.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs test/scripts/check-deadcode-unused-files.test.ts`
- `git diff --check`
- `corepack pnpm openclaw --version` -> `OpenClaw 2026.6.2 (c604b58)`

## Real behavior proof
Behavior addressed: OpenClaw-native provider failures that exhaust fallback now produce a final lifecycle error signal, so the gateway clears the webchat run and persists terminal failed session state immediately instead of leaving the session in progress/running.

Real environment tested: Patched local checkout `/Users/andy/openclaw-91730-provider-failure` on macOS, head `c604b584263bd554d5246dd5d8437b48add0aa4f`, build `OpenClaw 2026.6.2 (c604b58)`, isolated temp `OPENCLAW_HOME=/tmp/openclaw-91730-proof-cifix-IYxcUk`, gateway on loopback port `18796`, token auth with a throwaway proof token, default dev agent model `openai/gpt-5.5`, and deliberately invalid `OPENAI_API_KEY=sk-openc*************alid`. The provider failure used the real embedded runtime against OpenAI Responses websocket/HTTP endpoints; no provider mock was used for this proof.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: Started the temp gateway with `OPENCLAW_HOME=/tmp/openclaw-91730-proof-cifix-IYxcUk OPENAI_API_KEY=<invalid> corepack pnpm openclaw gateway run --dev --reset --port 18796 --auth token --token <throwaway> --tailscale off --compact --verbose`. Connected a loopback backend WebSocket client with shared-token auth and scopes `operator.read,operator.write`, verified `health` and an empty `sessions.list`, then sent `chat.send` with `sessionKey=agent:dev:main`, `message=PR91895_PROOF_TRIGGER provider failure lifecycle ci-fix`, and `idempotencyKey=pr91895-proof-cifix-20260610T0231`. After the terminal chat event, queried `sessions.list`, `chat.history`, and `health`, then stopped the temp gateway cleanly.

Evidence after fix: Gateway logs showed the real runtime starting the turn and contacting OpenAI, then failing on invalid auth:

```text
OpenClaw 2026.6.2 (c604b58)
[ws] Ōćä res Ō£ō chat.send 5ms runId=pr91895-proof-cifix-20260610T0231
[diagnostic] session turn created: runId=pr91895-proof-cifix-20260610T0231 sessionId=354fa727-3b28-46a9-9045-f52efd334c1b sessionKey=agent:dev:main agentId=dev channel=webchat trigger=user
[agent/embedded] ... failed to connect to websocket: HTTP error: 401 Unauthorized, url: wss://api.openai.com/v1/responses
[agent/embedded] ... Incorrect API key provided: sk-openc*************alid ... auth error code: invalid_api_key
[ws] -> event agent ... stream=lifecycle ... phase=error
[diagnostic] session state: sessionId=354fa727-3b28-46a9-9045-f52efd334c1b sessionKey=agent:dev:main prev=processing new=idle reason="run_completed" queueDepth=0
[diagnostic] run cleared: sessionId=354fa727-3b28-46a9-9045-f52efd334c1b totalActive=0
[model-fallback/decision] model fallback decision: decision=candidate_failed requested=openai/gpt-5.5 candidate=openai/gpt-5.5 reason=rate_limit next=none detail=unexpected status 401 Unauthorized ... auth error code: invalid_api_key
[ws] -> event agent ... stream=lifecycle ... phase=fallback_step
[ws] -> event agent ... stream=lifecycle ... phase=error
[diagnostic] message processed: channel=webchat ... sessionKey=agent:dev:main outcome=completed
[shutdown] completed cleanly in 110ms
```

The proof client observed the terminal chat event:

```json
{
  "chatSendAck": { "runId": "pr91895-proof-cifix-20260610T0231", "status": "started" },
  "terminalChatEvent": {
    "runId": "pr91895-proof-cifix-20260610T0231",
    "sessionKey": "agent:dev:main",
    "state": "error",
    "errorMessage": "unexpected status 401 Unauthorized: Incorrect API key provided: sk-openc*************alid..."
  }
}
```

A settled `sessions.list` after `message_completed` showed terminal failed state and no active run:

```json
{
  "key": "agent:dev:main",
  "sessionId": "354fa727-3b28-46a9-9045-f52efd334c1b",
  "status": "failed",
  "startedAt": 1781083947833,
  "endedAt": 1781083966242,
  "runtimeMs": 18409,
  "modelProvider": "openai",
  "model": "gpt-5.5",
  "agentRuntime": { "id": "codex", "source": "implicit" },
  "deliveryContext": { "channel": "webchat" },
  "lastChannel": "webchat",
  "hasActiveRun": false
}
```

The same proof client also saw `healthBefore.ok=true`, `healthAfter.ok=true`, `healthAfter.sessionCount=1`, `historySummary.ok=true`, and `historySummary.messageCount=1`.

Observed result after fix: The live temp-gateway turn produced a visible terminal `chat` error event, cleared the active run (`totalActive=0`), and persisted the session as `status:"failed"` with `endedAt`, `runtimeMs`, and `hasActiveRun:false`. Health remained OK after the failure, and the temp proof gateway was stopped cleanly after the proof.

What was not tested: I did not reproduce the exact reporter environment of Linux plus OpenAI OAuth on `openai/gpt-5.4-mini`; the proof uses the same OpenClaw-native provider failure class with a deliberately invalid OpenAI API key so it can be reproduced safely without live credentials. At the time of this final proof update, GitHub Actions and ClawSweeper re-review still need to run on the amended head.

target.title: doctor(config): auto-prune removed google-antigravity-auth entries
target.body:
## Summary

Describe the problem and fix in 2ŌĆō5 bullets:

- Problem: users can keep stale `plugins.entries["google-antigravity-auth"]` config after the plugin was removed, which keeps emitting warnings (`#27371`).
- Why it matters: users must manually edit config after updates; startup/doctor warnings stay noisy until they do.
- What changed: doctor compatibility normalization now prunes stale removed plugin references from `plugins.entries`, `plugins.installs`, `plugins.allow`, `plugins.load.paths`, and `plugins.slots.memory`.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): no provider runtime/auth behavior changes; this only cleans stale config references.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor
- [ ] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [ ] Integrations
- [ ] API / contracts
- [x] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #27371
- Related #13141

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

`openclaw doctor --fix` now automatically removes stale `google-antigravity-auth` plugin config references instead of requiring manual cleanup.

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? (`No`)
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? (`No`)
- New/changed network calls? (`No`)
- Command/tool execution surface changed? (`No`)
- Data access scope changed? (`No`)
- If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation:

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: macOS
- Runtime/container: Node 22 + pnpm
- Model/provider: N/A
- Integration/channel (if any): N/A
- Relevant config (redacted): `plugins.entries["google-antigravity-auth"]` present

### Steps

1. Add stale `google-antigravity-auth` plugin references under `plugins.*`.
2. Run `openclaw doctor --fix`.
3. Re-check config and doctor output.

### Expected

- Stale removed plugin references are pruned automatically.

### Actual

- Confirmed via migration test coverage.

## Evidence

Attach at least one:

- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after
- [ ] Trace/log snippets
- [ ] Screenshot/recording
- [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant)

## Human Verification (required)

What you personally verified (not just CI), and how:

- Verified scenarios: ran `pnpm test src/commands/doctor-legacy-config.migrations.test.ts` and verified new migration test passes.
- Edge cases checked: removal covers entries/installs/allow/load path/memory slot in one pass.
- What you did **not** verify: full end-to-end doctor run on a live upgraded host.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? (`Yes`)
- Config/env changes? (`No`)
- Migration needed? (`No`)
- If yes, exact upgrade steps:

## Failure Recovery (if this breaks)

- How to disable/revert this change quickly: revert this PR.
- Files/config to restore: `src/commands/doctor-legacy-config.ts`.
- Known bad symptoms reviewers should watch for: unintended plugin config pruning outside removed plugin IDs.

## Risks and Mitigations

List only real risks for this PR. Add/remove entries as needed. If none, write `None`.

- Risk: accidental over-pruning of plugin config if removal targeting expands incorrectly.
  - Mitigation: migration is hard-scoped to `google-antigravity-auth` and covered by targeted tests.

AI-assisted: yes.

target.title: feat(hooks): add agent and tool lifecycle boundaries
target.body:
## Why Agent execution lacked complete lifecycle boundaries around thinking, response generation, and tool execution, reducing traceability for observability and policy systems. This PR adds those boundaries and ensures tool lifecycle hooks carry `toolCallId` context when available. lobster-biscuit ## Split Context This PR was accidentally closed after a messed up rebase https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/12583 which was originally split from closed umbrella PR #9761: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/9761. ## Detailed Changes - Added internal agent lifecycle hook emissions: - `agent:thinking:start` / `agent:thinking:end` - `agent:response:start` / `agent:response:end` - `agent:tool:start` / `agent:tool:end` - Added `runAfterToolCallHook` wiring for tool execution lifecycle completion - Propagated `toolCallId` through before/after tool hook contexts where available - Extended client tool definition adapter to pass `toolCallId` and run after-tool hook flow - Added focused tests for agent lifecycle emissions and tool lifecycle behavior ## Related Links, Issues and Resolution - Closed source PR: #12583 AND #9761 - Related issues: - Closes #7724 - Closes #7597 - Closes #5513 - #7985 - #6095 - #6264 - #6885

Greptile Summary

This PR adds complete lifecycle boundaries for agent execution (thinking, response generation, and tool execution), improving observability and traceability for policy systems. The implementation propagates `toolCallId` through tool lifecycle hooks and ensures consistent hook firing across different execution paths. Key improvements: - Split lifecycle hook logic into dedicated `lifecycle-hooks.ts` module - Ensured `agent:thinking:start/end` fire for all agent runs - Added conditional `agent:response:start/end` hooks that only fire when response output is generated - Propagated `toolCallId` to `before_tool_call` and `after_tool_call` hooks - Added `runAfterToolCallHook` wiring for both success and error paths in tool execution - Comprehensive test coverage validates hook emissions in different scenarios

Confidence Score: 5/5

- Safe to merge - well-structured refactoring with comprehensive test coverage - The changes are well-architected with proper error handling throughout. The new `lifecycle-hooks.ts` module cleanly separates concerns, all hook emission paths include error handling that logs warnings without failing execution, and the test coverage validates the core lifecycle hook behavior. The `toolCallId` propagation is handled consistently with proper fallbacks. - No files require special attention Last reviewed commit: d48824c

target.title: fix(agents): keep exec visible for lean local models
target.body:
Refs #86632.

## Summary

Lean local-model mode now keeps `exec` directly visible when Tool Search compacts the rest of the tool catalog. This gives small coding-tuned local models an obvious shell fallback for simple live-data or workspace probes instead of forcing them to search for a domain-specific tool and stop when none exists.

This deliberately does not add a `cmd` alias or automatically route weather/live-data requests through shell; those are broader behavior and security decisions.

## Verification

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/local-model-lean.test.ts src/agents/tool-search.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.tool-search-run-plan.test.ts`
- post-rebase: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/local-model-lean.test.ts src/agents/tool-search.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.tool-search-run-plan.test.ts`
- `node scripts/format-docs.mjs docs/concepts/experimental-features.md docs/gateway/local-models.md docs/providers/ollama.md`
- `git diff --check`
- AWS Crabbox: `corepack pnpm check:changed` on `cbx_58160a36c5b3` / `run_e0befbd1db32` (exit 0)
- autoreview: clean, no accepted/actionable findings

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: lean local-model Tool Search no longer hides `exec`, so small coding models can directly discover shell execution while heavier/default tools remain removed or cataloged.
Real environment tested: local linked OpenClaw worktree plus AWS Crabbox Linux runner `cbx_58160a36c5b3`.
Exact steps or command run after this patch: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/local-model-lean.test.ts src/agents/tool-search.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.tool-search-run-plan.test.ts`; `corepack pnpm check:changed` remotely through Crabbox.
Evidence after fix: focused tests passed locally after rebase; Crabbox changed gate completed successfully with exit 0.
Observed result after fix: Tool Search keeps predicate-excluded tools visible, and lean mode passes that predicate so `exec` remains in the direct tool list.
What was not tested: a live Ollama/Qwen weather prompt; this patch is the narrow catalog/visibility fix, not an automatic live-data routing policy.

target.title: fix(memory): increase QMD embedTimeoutMs default to 600s for local GGŌĆ”
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: Local GGUF models (e.g., embeddinggemma-300m) take 3-4 minutes to embed a 30-50 file workspace on commodity hardware (4-core CPU), but `memory.qmd.update.embedTimeoutMs` default is only 120s, causing repeated timeout failures and effectively disabling vector search.
- Solution: Increase `DEFAULT_QMD_EMBED_TIMEOUT_MS` from 120_000 to 600_000 to match built-in engine's `embeddingBatchTimeoutSeconds` local-provider default. Improve error message to surface config path: `memory.qmd.update.embedTimeoutMs`.
- What changed: 3 files ŌĆö `backend-config.ts` (default change), `qmd-manager.ts` (error message), `backend-config.test.ts` (test expectation).
- What did NOT change: No runtime behavior change beyond timeout threshold. No config schema change. No API surface change.

## Change Type (select all)
 
- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [ ] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [ ] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [x] Memory / storage
- [ ] Integrations
- [ ] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Fixes #74204
- Related #60856 (Ollama embeddings timeout, already fixed)
- [ ] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Motivation

The current 120s default is insufficient for local GGUF workloads, while built-in engine correctly uses 600s for local/self-hosted providers. Users discover the fix (`embedTimeoutMs: 600000`) only via `openclaw config schema` or docs, and error message does not hint at the config path. This PR aligns defaults and surfaces the fix in the error message.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior addressed: Increase QMD embed timeout default from 120s to 600s; improve error message to mention config path.
- Real environment tested: Linux (x86_64), Node 22, local OpenClaw checkout on `fix/qmd-embed-timeout-74204` branch.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:

grep -n "DEFAULT_QMD_EMBED_TIMEOUT_MS" packages/memory-host-sdk/src/host/backend-config.ts

grep -n "qmd embed failed" extensions/memory-core/src/memory/qmd-manager.ts

node scripts/run-vitest.mjs packages/memory-host-sdk/src/host/backend-config.test.ts -t "embedTimeoutMs"

- Evidence after fix:

```
$ grep -n "DEFAULT_QMD_EMBED_TIMEOUT_MS" packages/memory-host-sdk/src/host/backend-config.ts
102:const DEFAULT_QMD_EMBED_TIMEOUT_MS = 600_000;

$ grep -n "qmd embed failed" extensions/memory-core/src/memory/qmd-manager.ts
1800:    log.warn(
1801:      `qmd embed failed (${reason}): ${String(err)} ŌĆö consider increasing memory.qmd.update.embedTimeoutMs (default: 600000); backing off for ${Math.ceil(delayMs / 1000)}s`,
1802:    );

$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs packages/memory-host-sdk/src/host/backend-config.test.ts -t "embedTimeoutMs"
Ō£ō backend-config.test.ts (1 test) 1 passed
```

- Observed result after fix: Default changed from 120_000 to 600_000. Error message now includes config path hint. Test expectation updated and passed.
- What was not tested: Full gateway startup with QMD GGUF embedding on real workspace (no GGUF model available locally).

## Root Cause (if applicable)

N/A ŌĆö config default mismatch, not a bug in runtime logic.

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

N/A ŌĆö default change, no behavior regression risk.

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

Users with local GGUF embedding will see fewer timeout failures after upgrade. Error message now hints at config path if timeout still occurs.

## Diagram (if applicable)

```
Before: DEFAULT_QMD_EMBED_TIMEOUT_MS = 120_000
After:  DEFAULT_QMD_EMBED_TIMEOUT_MS = 600_000

Error message before: "qmd embed failed (...) ŌĆö backing off for ..."
Error message after:  "qmd embed failed (...) ŌĆö consider increasing memory.qmd.update.embedTimeoutMs (default: 600000); backing off for ..."
```

## Security Impact (required)

- [ ] New permissions/capabilities? No
- [ ] Secrets/tokens handling changed? No
- [ ] New/changed network calls? No
- [ ] Command/tool execution surface changed? No
- [ ] Data access scope changed? No

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: Linux x86_64
- Runtime/container: Node 22
- Model/provider: N/A
- Integration/channel: QMD memory
- Relevant config (redacted): N/A

### Steps

1. Checkout `fix/qmd-embed-timeout-74204` branch
2. Verify default changed via grep
3. Verify error message updated via grep
4. Run test expectation

### Expected

`DEFAULT_QMD_EMBED_TIMEOUT_MS` is 600_000. Error message mentions `memory.qmd.update.embedTimeoutMs`. Test passes.

### Actual

All checks pass as expected.

## Evidence

- [ ] Failing test/log before + passing after
- [x] Trace/log snippets (grep output)
- [ ] Screenshot/recording
- [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant)

## Human Verification (required)

- Verified scenarios: Default value change, error message update, test expectation alignment
- Edge cases checked: N/A (simple config change)
- What you did NOT verify: Live gateway startup with QMD GGUF embedding

## Review Conversations

- [ ] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [ ] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

## Compatibility / Migration

- [x] Backward compatible? Yes
- [ ] Config/env changes? No (default only)
- [ ] Migration needed? No

## Risks and Mitigations

None ŌĆö simple default increase aligning with built-in engine's local provider default.

Fixes #74204

target.title: feat(ui): add KaTeX math rendering support
target.body:
## Summary

Add KaTeX-based mathematical formula rendering to OpenClaw chat interface. When `ui.mathRendering` is set to `"katex"`, LaTeX math expressions (both inline `$...$` and display `$$...$$`) are rendered as beautifully formatted math in chat messages.

## Changes

### Configuration
- Add `mathRendering?: "off" | "katex"` to `types.openclaw.ts` UI config
- Add corresponding Zod schema in `zod-schema.ts` (inside `.strict()` ui object)
- Add `ui.mathRendering` label in `schema.labels.ts`, help text in `schema.help.ts`, documentation in `configuration-reference.md`

### Core Rendering
- New `ui/src/ui/katex-renderer.ts` module with math extraction, pre-processing, and synchronous rendering
- Placeholder-based architecture: extract math ŌåÆ sanitize HTML ŌåÆ restore rendered math
- Handles `\$` escaped delimiters, nested braces, display/inline modes
- Post-processes `katex-error` class ŌåÆ falls back to raw LaTeX text
- CJK boundary support: `isCJKBoundary()` covers fullwidth punctuation, CJK punctuation, CJK ideographs, Hangul, Kana
- Markdown formatting boundary support: `*`, `_`, `~` after closing `$` recognized as valid boundaries

### DOMPurify Integration
- New `toSanitizedMarkdownHtmlWithKatex()` function (original `toSanitizedMarkdownHtml` unchanged)
- `uponSanitizeAttribute` DOMPurify hook using `node.closest(".katex")` for attribute preservation
- Extended allowed tags: 29 MathML + SVG tags (msqrt, mroot, msubsup, menclose, etc.)
- Extended allowed attributes: 36 MathML + SVG attributes (mathvariant, displaystyle, preserveAspectRatio, viewBox, etc.)

### Rendering Pipeline
- `grouped-render.ts`: Thread `mathRendering` through `renderMarkdownContent` selector and `renderStreamingGroup`
- Streaming: `mathRendering` passed through `renderStreamingGroup()` ŌåÆ `renderGroupedMessage()`
- `chat.ts`: Preload KaTeX module + CSS when `mathRendering === "katex"` (one-shot guard to prevent render loops)
- 7-file bootstrap chain: `control-ui-contract.ts` ŌåÆ `control-ui.ts` ŌåÆ `control-ui-bootstrap.ts` ŌåÆ `app-view-state.ts` ŌåÆ `app.ts` ŌåÆ `app-render.ts` ŌåÆ `chat.ts`

### Tests
- 87 test cases in `katex-renderer.test.ts` covering extraction, rendering, escaping, error handling, CJK boundaries, Markdown formatting boundaries, maxSize cap, XSS protection
- All UI tests pass, pre-existing failures in googlechat/matrix extensions are unrelated to this PR

### Dependencies
- Pinned `katex@0.16.47` (exact version, zero behavioral impact)

## Design Decisions

- **Full synchronous architecture**: No async changes to `toSanitizedMarkdownHtml`; new sync `toSanitizedMarkdownHtmlWithKatex` function
- **Streaming safety**: KaTeX rendering now flows through streaming path via `mathRendering` parameter
- **CJK boundary support**: Closing `$` before fullwidth punctuation, CJK characters, Hangul, and Kana is recognized as valid boundary
- **Markdown formatting boundary support**: Closing `$` before `*`, `_`, `~` (bold, italic, strikethrough markers) is recognized as valid boundary ŌĆö fixes `**$x^2$**` not rendering
- **Error resilience**: KaTeX parse errors gracefully fall back to displaying raw LaTeX; `maxSize: 100` prevents layout-breaking output
- **Render loop guard**: `katexPreloadTriggered` one-shot boolean prevents infinite update cycles
- **Zero breakage**: All existing callers of `toSanitizedMarkdownHtml` continue working unchanged

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior or issue addressed**: KaTeX math rendering in OpenClaw Control UI chat. Inline and display LaTeX formulas render correctly. CJK boundary formulas like `$x^2$ńÜäÕĆ╝` and `$E=mc^2$’╝ī` render correctly. Markdown formatting boundary formulas like `**$2^{m \times n}$**` and `*$x^2$*` render correctly. Numeric inline math like `$5 and $10$` no longer rejected by currency regex.

**Real environment tested**: OpenClaw Control UI dev server (`pnpm dev:ui:mock`) with `mathRendering: "katex"` enabled, tested in Chrome browser on Linux. Also verified on live OpenClaw gateway (systemd service, port 18789) with production config.

**Exact steps or command run after this patch**:
1. Set `mathRendering: "katex"` in config
2. Start OpenClaw (dev: `pnpm dev:ui:mock` or gateway: `pnpm gateway:watch`)
3. Open Chrome, navigate to Control UI
4. Send chat messages containing LaTeX formulas:
   - Basic: `$E=mc^2$`, `$$\int_0^1 f(x)dx$$`
   - CJK boundary: `$x^2$ńÜäÕĆ╝`, `$E=mc^2$’╝īõ╗źÕÅŖ`
   - Markdown formatting: `**$2^{m \times n}$**`, `*$x^2$*`, `~~$a+b$~~`
   - Numeric content: `$5 and $10$`
5. Observe rendered output in chat window ŌĆö all formulas render correctly

**Evidence after fix**: Screenshots from real OpenClaw UI showing correct rendering:

Inline and display math rendering:
![Inline and display math](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/85a4a034-b275-4510-8d19-f66b9cf4b03e)

Complex formulas (matrices, integrals, nested):
![Complex formulas](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/2a75c1c0-50de-4452-a862-fd16e2d27936)

CJK boundary formulas (fullwidth comma, CJK ideograph):
![CJK boundary formulas](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/98b80524-6179-4e3a-af53-7f36ea766e51)

Additional formula rendering (fractions, roots, Greek letters):
![Additional formulas](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/650da05c-14e0-4b5d-ab21-79022feadcfb)

**Observed result after fix**: All LaTeX formulas render correctly in the OpenClaw chat interface ŌĆö inline math, display math, complex nested formulas, CJK boundary cases, and Markdown formatting boundary cases. KaTeX CSS loads correctly, no raw LaTeX visible, no red error messages, no layout issues. Numeric inline math (e.g. `$5 and $10$`) no longer rejected by false currency detection.

**What was not tested**: Actual AI model integration (requires API key). Multi-session switching while KaTeX is loaded. Very long formula chains that might trigger maxSize fallback.
